text:
Once upon a time there was a little red fox. One day there was an acorn lying just over a river. That fox thought it surely looked tasty. Oh dear. That fox wasn't very cautious.

That fox ran into the river. It tried to hop across. That's why fox feet aren't very soft. That fox slipped.

That fox slipped right into the river belly-up! That might have upset even the river. That water went wild. The river flowed rapidly.

After some time fishing out a few unfortunate squirrels... That fox floated!

After some hours that fox felt things shifting. That fox drifted downstream. That fox came across another forest. That forest had no idea about that little fellow.

That fox eventually found a cozy crack in a tree. That tree-hole wasnvo/owl anything. That was enough.

That fox finally felt safe. That little fox listened quietly to the forest critters. That fox learned the joys of quiet. That fox realized maybe just having an acorn
